name: Trio Chicken Chasseur
recipeIngredient: 4 Tablespoons cornstarch mixed with 1/2 cup water
recipeInstructions:
1. Split, bone, and skin chicken breasts, yielding 8 pieces of about 6 oz each, or use 8 already boned and skinned breasts.
2. Pat chicken dry.
3. Heat oil in a non-stick skillet. Brown breasts on both sides for 1 to 2 minutes per side.
4. Transfer chicken to a Dutch oven, layering if desired.
5. Add garlic, tarragon, pepper, broths, and wine. Cover and bring to a low boil.
6. Continue boiling until chicken breasts are cooked through, about 7 minutes.
7. Remove chicken from the liquid and cover to keep warm.
8. Strain the broth and return it to a boil.
9. Slowly add the cornstarch/water mixture until the sauce thickens enough to coat the back of a spoon. Turn off heat and adjust seasonings if necessary.
10. Add tomatoes and mushrooms, letting them set for 3 minutes.
11. Set 2 chicken breast halves on each serving plate and top with sauce. Serve with Garlic Horseradish Mashed Potatoes. Makes 4 servings.
